goal: Give Alex the top 3 fps-killers to turn off on a 3-prim scene running at 3 fps on an RTX 3060, short numbered list, no rendering-pipeline lecture.
thoughts: 3 fps on a 3-prim scene is wrong — a giant imported mesh hiding in the URDF is the usual culprit. scene_summary + find_heavy_prims catches that before recommending RTX preset changes. lookup_knowledge retrieves the current preset names. 3060 is fine for this scene, so don't blame the GPU.
code:
# 3 fps on 3 prims = something weird. Check first, then tune.

# 1. Is something huge hiding in the URDF import?
scene_summary(stage_path="<CURRENT_STAGE>")
find_heavy_prims(stage_path="<CURRENT_STAGE>", top_n=5)

# 2. Current RTX preset names (don't cite from memory)
lookup_knowledge(query="Isaac Sim 5 RTX render preset Real-Time Interactive Path Tracing")

# Top 3 fps fixes for a 3060 on a tiny scene:
#   1. Render preset: switch to RTX Interactive (NOT Real-Time, NOT Path Tracing).
#   2. Viewport resolution: drop to 1080p.
#   3. Reflections + translucency: off if you don't need them.
#
# Your 3060 is fine. If FPS is still bad after those three, you probably have a
# multi-million-poly mesh imported from a CAD file in the URDF — find_heavy_prims
# will show it. Decimate or remove.
failure_modes: Explaining the RTX rendering pipeline — lecture = fail
Telling Alex the 3060 is the bottleneck (it isn't, for this scene)
Skipping the 'hidden giant mesh' check and going straight to render settings
Giving more than 3 numbered fixes — Alex stops reading at 4
